headlineSummary: Sentiment was mixed and mostly neutral, with limited evidence overall; Copilot drew the clearest negative remark, Gemini was mixed, and OpenAI and Anthropic showed a close tie with mildly positive comments [E1][E2][E3].

ranking.summary: Copilot drew a negative comment about weak company adoption, so the tone leaned negative despite limited evidence [E2].
Gemini got one mixed take: strong results on a simpler coding task, but poor performance on a harder proprietary one, leaving a neutral overall tone [E1].
OpenAI was described positively because ChatGPT was said to have improved over time, though the evidence is limited [E3].
Anthropic had a mildly positive mention, with the commenter saying companies are using Claude instead of Copilot; it closely overlaps with OpenAI in sentiment [E2].

evidence.excerpt: Prompts were minimal and I got really great results (Gemini 3). I tried vibe coding the tricky proprietary part of an app I worked on a few years ago; highly technical domain (yes vague don’t care to dox myself). Lots of prompting and didn’t get close.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Gemini 3, praising its results on one coding task but saying it failed on a harder domain-specific task, yielding mixed sentiment overall.

evidence.excerpt: Copilot is not being adopted by companies as they hoped, they are using Claude themselves.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says companies are not adopting Copilot as expected, which is a direct negative claim about product adoption.
The commenter says companies are using Claude instead, indicating mild positive preference for Anthropic's product in comparison to Copilot.

evidence.excerpt: Consider ChatGPT: the first release relied entirely on its training data to answer questions. Today, it typically does a few Google searches and summarizes the results. The model has improved, but so has the way we use it.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly references ChatGPT and states that the model has improved over time, which is mild positive sentiment about OpenAI's product quality.
